name: SubspaceIter3FromRandomB
description: Pure subspace iteration from random orthogonal B. Freq=3. Random init spreads energy across all eigenvectors for faster convergence than identity.
author: GLM-5
result: Pure subspace iteration from random orthogonal B, freq=3. No eigendecomposition at all. Random orthogonal initialization converges slightly faster than identity by spreading energy across all eigenvectors. Key improvements: 1) No eigh initialization (eigh on rank-1 A was counterproductive), 2) Random orthogonal B instead of identity, 3) freq=3 optimal. Score formula heavily weights explained variance^10, so even small ev improvements matter.
